plan_adherence_notes: Against ff/Tbayes-market-qg9, the execution matches the planned task shape: frontend/src/lib/api/client.ts now normalizes and serializes status/sort/q, frontend/src/lib/query/hooks.ts widens the markets query key and preserves list-wide invalidation via queryKeys.marketLists(), frontend/src/routes/MarketList.tsx reads and writes filters through URL search params with a 300 ms debounced search commit, and frontend tests were added for API serialization, query keys, and MarketList interactions. Local verification shows pnpm --dir frontend test passes (52 tests). pnpm --dir frontend build still fails, but the errors are in inherited Analytics code outside this task diff (missing analytics component modules and implicit-any callbacks in frontend/src/routes/Analytics.tsx), so the market filtering/search implementation itself adheres to the plan even though the branch is not build-clean end to end.
missed_questions: When search state moves into the URL, should status/sort changes preserve the user's pending uncommitted search text or only the last debounced committed term?
What browser-history behavior is expected for these filter updates (push vs replace), given that shareable/back-forward URL state was a core reason for the design?
reason: The branch closes the concrete gaps from the prior bayes-market-qg9 validation: status, sort, and q are now wired through the client, React Query cache keys, MarketList URL state, and frontend tests. The only failing verification I found is an Analytics build problem outside this branch's task-specific diff, so I would approve the market filtering/search UI work while tracking that inherited build issue separately.
